name: Breakable Object
description: Breakable Object is a world-system mechanic driven by forces, constraints, collision response, or simulated motion.
design_notes: Breakable Object should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For physics mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use breakable object as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect breakable object to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels the world has weight, motion, and consequences.

parameters.name: force_strength
parameters.description: Magnitude of the force, impulse, or constraint effect.

parameters.name: mass_or_weight
parameters.description: Mass scale used by collision and force calculations.

parameters.name: damping
parameters.description: How quickly motion loses energy over time.

edge_cases: Breakable Object is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Breakable Object overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Breakable Object runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Breakable Object state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Breakable Object plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Breakable Object produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Breakable Object strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Breakable Object becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Breakable Object with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Breakable Object as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Breakable Object with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Breakable Object as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
